finding_title: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.
finding_description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).

  Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 18.2px instead of at least 24px.

finding_title: Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names.
finding_description: Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. [Learn more about accessible names](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label-content-name-mismatch).

finding_title: `[role]`s do not have all required `[aria-*]` attributes
finding_description: Some ARIA roles have required attributes that describe the state of the element to screen readers. [Learn more about roles and required attributes](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/aria-required-attr).
item_explanation:
Fix any of the following:
  Required ARIA attribute not present: aria-level

finding_title: Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order
finding_description: Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. [Learn more about heading order](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/heading-order).

finding_title: Links rely on color to be distinguishable.
finding_description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. [Learn how to make links distinguishable](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/link-in-text-block).

  The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

finding_title: Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`).
finding_description: Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. [Learn more about proper list structure](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/list).

  List element has direct children that are not allowed: [role=button]

finding_title: Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0
finding_description: A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. [Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/tabindex).
